essentials.description: Active: Active - Azure Managed Redis metric reporting issue

properties.title: Active - Azure Managed Redis metric reporting issue
properties.communication:
Impact Statement: Starting at 14:00 UTC on 20 May 2026, you have been identified as a customer using Azure Managed Redis who may experience incorrect or higher-than-expected or inaccurate Server Load metrics displayed in the monitoring dashboards. On smaller SKUs (2-vCPU nodes), customers may experience brief latency spikes during these intervals.

 

Workaround:

  • As an alternative to Server Load, use the CPU metric (percentProcessorTime). This metric is less sensitive to the reporting issue because it uses an improved calculation that filters out short-lived CPU spikes and provides a more accurate representation of sustained CPU utilization. While the CPU metric is also affected by this reporting issue, the impact is significantly lower compared to Server Load.
  • When viewing the Server Load or CPU metric in the Azure portal, use the Average aggregation instead of Max to obtain a more representative view of resource utilization.
  • Until this issue is resolved, any scaling decisions should be based on the CPU metric (percentProcessorTime) rather than the Server Load metric.
  • If you are experiencing latency impact on a smaller SKU, temporary mitigations are available on request, please contact Azure Support referencing this advisory.

 

Current Status:

We are rolling out a fleet-wide short-term mitigation that staggers the internal processes responsible for the periodic, short-lived CPU spikes, which in turn reduces the Server Load spikes previously observed. This mitigation has now been applied to more than 98% of the fleet, with only a small number of caches remaining. We expect the fleet-wide mitigation to be fully completed by 21 July 2026.

Once applied, this mitigation significantly reduces the periodic spikes. Please note that, depending on the cache SKU, customers may still observe some reduced Server Load/CPU spikes that no longer reach 100%; this is expected behavior for the current mitigation. The cache remains healthy with no sustained CPU pressure, errors, or resource exhaustion.

A permanent fix that will eliminate these spikes completely is scheduled to be deployed during our maintenance cycle in August 2026.

The next update will be provided on 22 July 2026, or as events warrant.
